SESSION.NAME: defaultSession
SESSION.DESCRIPTION: This diagnostic session enables the default diagnostic session in the server(s) and does not support any diagnostic application timeout handling provisions (e.g.., TesterPresent service is not necessary to keep the session active). A server shall always start the default diagnostic session when powered up. If no other diagnostic session is started, then the default diagnostic session shall be running as long as the server is powered.

SESSION.NAME: programmingSession
SESSION.DESCRIPTION: This diagnosticSession enables all diagnostic services required to support the memory programming of a server. Regardless of whether the programmingSession runs in boot software or not, the ECU shall automatically suspend logging of DTCs and suspend transmission of non-diagnostic frames when entering the programmingSession.

SESSION.NAME: extendedDiagnosticSession
SESSION.DESCRIPTION: This diagnosticSession is used to enable timed diagnostic service functionality (e.g., I/O control). The diagnostic functionality in this session shall also include all supported diagnostic functionality supported in the defaultSession.

SECURITY_LEVEL.NAME: Send Key (Security Level 0x01)
SECURITY_LEVEL.DESCRIPTION: Security level is supported in ProgrammingDiagnosticSession and is required to reprogram.

SECURITY_LEVEL.NAME: Send Key (Security Level 0x03)
SECURITY_LEVEL.DESCRIPTION: Security level protects vulnerable diagnostic services.

DATA_FORMAT_IDENTIFIER.NAME: No Compression Method and No Encryption Method

DID.NAME: Differential Locking Actuator A Reported State

SUB_FIELD.NAME: Differential Locking Actuator A Reported State

ENUM_MEMBER.DESCRIPTION: Initialization - Differential Locking Actuator "A" software is not yet ready to process commands
Disconnected - Decoupled state allows the left and right wheels to have open differentiation
Connected - Locking mechanism couples the left and right wheels together and does not allow differentiation
In transition - Actuator changing current in the solenoid such that it is either below disconnected or over connected threshod
Blocked - A sensor has determined that the Actuators was not able to achieve the connected position, not available for T6
Fault - The software is not able to determine if the Differential Locking Actuator "A" is connected or disconnected

DID.NAME: Differential Locking Actuator B Reported State

SUB_FIELD.NAME: Differential Locking Actuator B Reported State

ENUM_MEMBER.DESCRIPTION: Initialization - Differential Locking Actuator "B" software is not yet ready to process commands
Disconnected - Decoupled state allows the left and right wheels to have open differentiation
Connected - Locking mechanism couples the left and right wheels together and does not allow differentiation
In Transition - Actuator changing current in the solenoid such that it is either below disconnected or over connected threshod
Blocked - A sensor has determined that the Actuator was not able to achieve the connected position, not available for T6
Fault - The software is not able to determine if the Differential Locking Actuator "B" is connected or disconnected

DTC_FAILURE_TYPE.DESCRIPTION: Circuit Short To Ground or Open
Value of Signal Protection Calculation Incorrect

DTC_SNAPSHOT_RECORD.DESCRIPTION: The first time since DTC information was cleared or the DTC was aged that DTC status bit 3 (confirmedDTC) is set to 1.

DTC_EXTENDED_DATA_RECORD.NAME: Operation cycle counter #1
DTC_EXTENDED_DATA_RECORD.DESCRIPTION: Number of operation cycles since the DTC fault detection counter last reached its maximum value of +127 (since DTC information was last cleared). All operation cycles, including those during which the test was not completed shall be included.

DTC_EXTENDED_DATA_RECORD.NAME: Operation cycle counter #2
DTC_EXTENDED_DATA_RECORD.DESCRIPTION: Number of operation cycles since the DTC fault detection counter last reached its maximum value of +127 (since DTC information was last cleared). The operation cycles during which the test was not completed shall be excluded

DTC_EXTENDED_DATA_RECORD.NAME: Operation cycle counter #3
DTC_EXTENDED_DATA_RECORD.DESCRIPTION: Number of operation cycles since the DTC fault detection counter first reached its maximum value of +127 (since DTC information was last cleared). All operation cycles, including those in which the test has not been completed shall be included.

DTC_EXTENDED_DATA_RECORD.NAME: Operation cycle counter #4
DTC_EXTENDED_DATA_RECORD.DESCRIPTION: Number of operation cycles during which the DTC fault detection counter reached its maximum value of +127 (since DTC information was last cleared).

DTC_EXTENDED_DATA_RECORD.NAME: DTC Fault Detection Counter
DTC_EXTENDED_DATA_RECORD.DESCRIPTION: The purpose of this counter is to provide a mechanism for filtering the results of a low-level fault detection process so that test results (pass and fail) can be qualified before setting any DTC status bits.

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Battery "B" State of Charge Performance

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Test runs all the time module is powered
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: Test fails if BCM is requesting HV-to-LV energy transfer, and either HV battery SOC is low or HV battery discharge power limit is too low to allow energy transfer.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: No HV to LV energy transfer will occur - if 12V battery is low, it will not be charged by HV system, and will eventually deplete.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: Low/depleted 12V battery, and low HV battery SOC or power limits (due to other causes).

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Engine Coolant Heater "A" Control Circuit Performance

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: PTC Heater has been operating for more than [ccp_ptcf_pwr_tout milliseconds], at CAL power level (above [ccp_ptcf_minp W]), no indication from ECM that heater loop/valve/pump has failed, vehicle speed is close to zero, and engine is not running (if engine present).
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: PTC Heater is determined to be failed due to power consumption and coolant temperatures
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: Engine may be run to provide heat to the driver instead of using the HV heater (for vehicles with a ICE engine), only in PHEV, and only in cold ambients.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: Possible engine runtime increased (for programs with ICE engine). Cabin heat performance impacted.

DTC.DESCRIPTION: A/C Refrigerant Pressure Sensor "A" Circuit Range/Performance

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Test runs when the following are true: The AC compressor has been off for longer than cal [ccp_eac_off_min_time = 60.0 minutes] AND The Ambient temperature is above cal [ccp_eac_pres_mon_min_amb = 10.0 degC] AND The AC compressor has just turned on.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: Test fails for 3 possible reasons: 1)A/C pressure sensor is reading implausibly high 2)A/C pressure sensor has in-range fault 3)A/C pressure is stuck out of bounds
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: AC compressor is disabled.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: MIL illuminated (for ICE engine programs). Disabling the AC compressor will prevent cooling of the HV battery which can cause lower power limits and reduced performance. Disabling the AC compressor will also prevent climate control functionality.

DTC.DESCRIPTION: A/C Refrigerant System "A" Charge Loss

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Test runs when the following are true: The AC compressor has been off for longer than cal [ccp_eac_off_min_time = 60.0 minutes] AND The Ambient temperature is above cal [ccp_eac_pres_mon_min_amb = 10.0 degC] AND The AC compressor has just turned on AND No other rationality faults have been detected in with the A/C Refrigerant Pressure Sensor.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: Test fails when measured AC refrigerant pressure is below cal [ccp_eac_pres_lim_low bar] while the compressor is running.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: A/C Compressor disabled.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: MIL illuminated (for ICE powertrains). Disabling the AC compressor will prevent cooling of the HV battery which can cause lower power limits and reduced performance. Disabling the AC compressor will also prevent climate control functionality.

DTC.DESCRIPTION: System Voltage

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Powerup timer is GREATER than time after powerup to start check (calibrateable, [hld_kapwr_putmr = 0.50]) AND Power Sustain Relay (PSR) Voltage (hardwire) is more than cal [hld_kapwr_psrmin = 8.20] AND 2.5V reference is ok
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_PERIOD: 16ms for fault detection, 100ms for DTC test
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: Test fails if 12V battery voltage is below [hld_kapwr_vmin = 5.950] V OR above [hld_kapwr_vmax = 19.0] V
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: No further action is taken by this detection
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: FHEV: yellow wrench, PHEV: MIL - check engine light

DTC.DESCRIPTION: System Voltage Low

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Details: Any one of the following being TRUE will result in an Entry State for this DTC: Entry conditions: 1) Powerup timer is GREATER than time after powerup to start check (calibrateable, [hld_psrlo_putmr = 0.50]) AND 2.5V reference is ok (HCU check) 2) 2.5VREF is in range AND Vehicle operating state is not a power-down AND cal time after power-up has passed (MGCU check). 3) Vehicle is not in Park, and is moving above a calibrateable speed threshold - 3kph (VMP_LV_ERR) (HCU check).
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: PSR voltage below calibrateable threshold for calibrateable time threshold NOTE: For this DTC, fault conditions will only be generated if HEV Power sustain relay command = TRUE(closed), OR the Hardwired Key_run indication is received (Pin A29), OR the hardwired Wakeup indication is received (Pin A38) Fault conditions: 1) Power Sustain Relay Voltage, hardwire is LESS than minimum PSR voltage for fault check (calibrateable, [hcu_v_psr_min = 7.80] V for 10ms]). 2) 12V PSR (PSRM) Under Voltage (MGCU condition in Powersplit: lower than 7.33V for 10ms at the MGCU; MGCU condition in MHT: lower than 6.339V for 10ms at the MGCU) 3) 12V PSR voltage is below [vmp_lv_thr = 9.50 V] for 100ms VMP_LV_ERR (HCU). (Hardware resets Microprocessor at 3.66V ) fault 209
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: For condition(s) in Pass/Fail Criteria section: 1) Vehicle Shutdown 2) MGCU action in Powersplit: temporarily disable motor, generator and VVC; If the fault intermittently sets and clears more than 7 times in 14 seconds, the fault action becomes permanent. MGCU action in MHT: temporarily disable motor If the fault intermittently sets and clears more than 7 times in 14 seconds, the fault action becomes permanent. 3) Vehicle Shutdown
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: on powersplit or BEV PDU HEV Hazard - Red Triangle and for plugin, MIL - Check engine

DTC.DESCRIPTION: System Voltage High

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Powerup timer is GREATER than time after powerup to start check (calibrateable, [hld_psrhi_putmr = 0.50] sec) AND 2.5V reference voltage is measured within acceptable range
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_PERIOD: 2ms for the fault detection, 100ms for the DTC test
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: Power Sustain Relay Voltage (hardwire) is GREATER than or EQUAL a calibrateable value [hcu_v_psr_max = 19.0] V
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: No further actions taken by this fault detection
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: FHEV: yellow wrench, PHEV: No fault indicator

DTC.DESCRIPTION: TCM Processor

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: For test to run, the time after powerup must be greater than a calibrateable threshold (~50msec), and 12V power supplied is inside a calibrateable range.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: This test will fail if any critical signals from the MGCU are undefined, or are not behaving as expected, such as operating modes, software version numbers, or software reset detected. Details: Fault conditions: If one or more of the following statements evaluates as TRUE, DTC will be generated. Fault conditions: 1) MGCU processor has gone through an unexpected reset while the vehicle was in a driving mode that exceeded a calibrateable time threshold. 2) Internal HCU software error - possible memory corruption 3) undefined Generator mode information received from MGCU 4) undefined Motor mode information received from MGCU 5) MGCU modes are not responding as expected to HCU commands 6) The MGCU has experienced more than 5 resets during any power cycle while in a driving mode, regardless of duration. 7) software mismatch between HCU and MGCU
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: All of the listed fault conditions will result in vehicle shutdown
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: on powersplit or BEV PDU HEV Hazard Lamp (Red Triangle) and for plugin, MIL - check engine

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: Test fails when a task overrun is detected. A task overrun is defined as a given task being scheduled while another instance of the task is already queued and waiting to run

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Internal Control Module Torque Performance

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: HEIPC runs continuously when in torque delivery mode.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_PERIOD: DTC evaluates at 100ms; fault evaluates at 10ms.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: Fails if HEIPC detects a torque hazard (excess accel, excess decel or UVM).
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: Request super-creep LOS and/or disable regen, depending on the torque hazard.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: MIL, Repair (wrench) light.

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Sensor Reference Voltage "A" Circuit Range/Performance

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Time after powerup is greater than cal threshold.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_PERIOD: Fault detection rate is 1ms in MGCU. Fault is evaluated every 16ms in HCU. DTC test is run every 100ms
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: MHT: Fault conditions: If the following Fault condition is TRUE for a calibrateable time ( 100ms), AND an unexplained processor reset occurs, the DTC will be generated for this Fault on the subsequent power-up of the module. If 2.5V reference voltage is measured outside of calibrateable range (MHT: less than 2.33V or greater than 2.67V), then test fails Powersplit: Fault conditions: If any one or more of the following the Fault conditions is (are) TRUE for a calibrateable time ( 100ms), AND an unexplained processor reset occurs, the DTC will be generated for this Fault on the subsequent power-up of the module. (1) If 2.5V reference voltage is measured outside of calibrateable range (Powersplit: less than 2.4V or greater than 2.6V), then test fails (2) If 5V A/D power supply voltage is measured outside of calibrateable range (Powersplit: less than 2.4V or greater than 2.6V), then test fails fault 204
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: Powersplit: No action other than recording a fault. MHT: Temporary shutdown of motor If the fault intermittently sets and clears more than 7 times in 14 seconds, the fault action becomes permanent.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: on powersplit or BEV PDU HEV Hazard - Red Triangle and for PLUGIN only, MIL - check engine

DIDS_SUPPORTED_BY_FREEZE_FRAME.DID_REFS: did_F404 did_F405 did_F40C did_F40D did_F41F did_F430 did_F431 did_F442 did_F449

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Sensor Reference Voltage "B" Circuit Range/Performance

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Powersplit Details: Entry conditions: The test will run if any of the following are TRUE (2 different fault detections feed this DTC): 1) Entry conditions for 1.3V power supply: Time after powerup is greater than cal threshold, PSR voltage is within cal range, 2.5V A/D reference volatge is within cal range, 5V A/D supply voltage is within cal range. 2) Entry conditions for 3.3V power supply: Time after powerup is greater than cal threshold, PSR voltage is within cal range, 2.5V A/D reference volatge is within cal range, 5V A/D supply voltage is within cal range MHT Details: Entry conditions: The test will run if any of the following are TRUE (2 different fault detections feed this DTC): 1) Entry conditions for 1.3V power supply: Time after powerup is greater than cal threshold, PSR voltage is within cal range, 2.5V A/D reference volatge is within cal range. 2) Entry conditions for 3.3V power supply: Time after powerup is greater than cal threshold, PSR voltage is within cal range, 2.5V A/D reference volatge is within cal range
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_PERIOD: MGCU: Fault is checked every 1ms. DTC test is run every 100ms
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: Internal power supply voltage out of range for any of the following power supplies: 1.3V processor supply, 3.3V processor supply, AND a processor reset occurs during the same power cycle. Details: Fault conditions: If any one or more of the following the Fault conditions is (are) TRUE for a calibrateable time ( 100ms), AND an unexplained processor reset occurs, the DTC will be generated for this Fault on the subsequent power-up of the module. Fault conditions (corresponding to entry conditions): 1) 1.3 volt microprocessor power supply measurement (calibrateable) for fault check is Powerplit: higher than 1.42V or lower than 1.36V; MHT: higher than 1.392V or lower than 1.274V (Hardware resets Microprocessor between 1.27V and 1.396V) 2) 3.3 volt volt microprocessor power supply measurement (calibrateable) for fault check is Powerplit: higher than 3.5V or lower than 3.2V ; MHT: higher than 3.627V or lower than 2.972V (Hardware resets Microprocessor between 2.86-2.97V) fault 205 fault 206
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: No Immediate action is taken for these faults - only a DTC/warning light will be set if these faults occur AND an unexplained processor reset happen during the same key cycle.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: FHEV: yellow wrench, PHEV: MIL

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: Motor resolver 5V power supply out of range greater than 5.3V or less than 4.7V - fault 264

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Sensor Reference Voltage "D" Circuit Range/Performance

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Powersplit: Time after powerup is greater than cal threshold, PSR voltage is within cal range, 2.5V A/D reference volatge is within cal range , 5V A/D supply voltage is within cal range. MHT: Time after powerup is greater than cal threshold, PSR voltage is within cal range, 2.5V A/D reference volatge is within cal range.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: Generator resolver 5V power supply out of range fault conditions: Powersplit: higher than 5.3V or lower than 4.7V for a calibrateable time (500ms) MHT: higher than 5.241V or lower than 4.759V for a calibrateable time (500ms) fault 264 (MHT) - fault 265 (Powersplit)
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: MGCU: No further action taken, only the fault is reported
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: FHEV: yellow wrench, PHEV: Check Engine (MIL)

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Sensor Power Supply "A" Circuit Low

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Powersplit: Powerup timer is GREATER than time after powerup to start check (calibrateable), 2.5V A/D referece voltage is within normal range, 5V A/D supply voltage is within cal range AND PSR voltage is within range. MHT: Powerup timer is GREATER than time after powerup to start check (calibrateable), 2.5V A/D referece voltage is within normal range AND PSR voltage is within range.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_PERIOD: MGCU: 10ms fault detection rate, 100ms for DTC test
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: If MGCU detects temperature sensor reference supply voltage is outside of calibrateable range Powerplit: higher than 5.3V or lower than 4.7V; MHT: higher than 5.315V or lower than 4.691V for a calibrateable time ( 1000ms), test fails fault 280
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: MGCU: No further action is taken by this detection other than reporting.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: FHEV: yellow wrench, PHEV: MIL - check engine light

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Internal Control Module Non-Volatile Random Access Memory (NVRAM) Error

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Runs anytime key is on
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: Fails if reading or writing to NVRAM backing store fails in a non-recoverable fashion.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: No additional action is taken by this fault detection
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: FHEV: no fault warning light, PHEV: Check Engine (MIL). Some diagnostic information may be lost.

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Transmission Fluid Temperature Sensor "A" Circuit Range/Performance

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: PowerUp timer is greater than cal [ldp_oilcoil_putmr] seconds AND PowerUp timer is less than cal [ldp_oilcoil_maxtm] AND mot/gen coil circuit status is ok AND minimum soak time for fault check [ldp_oilcoil_soak] has passed AND oil temperature status is ok AND 5V reference is ok AND key off timer is ok
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: Basically, the oil temperature sensor reads a temperature significantly different from the other two temperatures sensors in the transmission case (motor coil, generator coil) after a long soak time - when all 3 temperatures should match. This is an "in-range" failure of the sensor. Detailed Fault condition: (Difference between Oil Temp Sensor and MTR Coil Temp Sensor) more than cal [ldp_oilcoil_lim] degC AND (Difference between Oil Temp Sensor AND Gen Coil Temp Sensor) more than cal [ldp_oilcoil_lim] Deg C AND (Difference between Motor Coil Temp Sensor and gen Coil Temp Sensor) is less than cal [ldp_oilcoil_ok_dlt] deg C
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: No additional action is taken by this fault detection, but if the oil temp sensor is reading hotter than the real temperature, some motor/generator torque limiting could result that may result in more engine run time.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: FHEV: yellow wrench, PHEV: Check Engine (MIL). Fault could result in more engine run time.

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Transmission Fluid Temperature Sensor "A" Circuit Low

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Time after powerup must be greater than cal threshold, PSR voltage within normal range, 5V reference supply within range, 2.5V reference supply within range, motor and generator stator temperatures above a threshold (20 deg C) and vehicle mode is not VM_INIT for the test to run.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: Fault conditions- Oil temperature sensor shorted to ground - as measured by HCU
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: This fault can limit motor and generator maximum torque limits and engine speed limits.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: FHEV: yellow wrench, PHEV: Check Engine (MIL)

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Transmission Fluid Temperature Sensor "A" Circuit High

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Time after powerup must be greater than cal threshold, PSR voltage within cal range, 5V reference supply within range, 2.5V reference supply within range, and vehicle mode is not VM_INIT for the test to run.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: Fault conditions: 1) Oil temperature sensor open circuit- as reported by HCU
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: 1) HCU response - No additional action is taken by this fault detection
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: FHEV: yellow wrench, PHEV: Check Engine (MIL). Fault could result in more engine run time.

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Transmission Fluid Temperature Sensor "A" Circuit Intermittent

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Time after powerup is greater than threshold (0.5 seconds) and 12V PSR voltage inside required range for module operation.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_PERIOD: Test runs every 2ms, DTC is evaluated every 100ms
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: Test fails if oil temp sensor rate of change exceeds a threshold (50 degC/sec) for at least 5 samples
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: Machine torque derating will use a default value, max component speeds can be reduced to fixed thresholds, and cooling system requests will be max (pump flow, fan speeds, etc)
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: No warning lights illuminated, potential small decreases in max performance.

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Transmission Mode Switch "A" Circuit

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Power up timer is greater than a cal value [hld_evsw_putmr = 0.50], PSR voltage within range.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: EV button - Intermittent switching - high frequency changes in EV button state suggest that an intermittent wiring concern is present, rather than the customer pushing the button. If too many changes in button state (on/off) are observed in a very short window of time (100ms), then this behavior is not likely the result of the customer pushing the button, but an intermittent wiring issue.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: Loss of EV mode operation in PHEV vehicles only, for that key cycle
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: Loss of EV-only mode operation - HEV repair (wrench) light

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Engine Speed Input Circuit Range/Performance

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Test will run if PSR voltage is within calibrateable range, engine speed CAN signal from ECM received successfully, Time after powerup is greater than cal threshold, and calculated engine speed is within calibrateable range. Details: Logical Entry conditions: All of the following Entry conditions (#1, #2, #3, #4, #5) must evaluate to TRUE in order to enter an Entry state. Entry conditions (from check_cto_rationality(void)): 1) PSR voltage in-range 2) Engine speed CAN signal is being received from ECM 3) Powerup timer is GREATER than calibrateable timer value ([pvp_cto_putmr = 0.50] seconds). 4) Engine speed is GREATER than minimum engine speed to check for CTO fault (calibrateable value, [pvp_cto_mnspd = 50.0] rad/sec) 5) Engine speed is LESS than maximum engine speed to check for CTO fault (calibrateable value, [pvp_cto_mxspd = 200.0] rad/sec)
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: Test will fail if the difference between PCM engine speed CAN signal and the engine speed calculated from CTO hardwired signal is greater than a calibrateable threshold [pvp_cto_mxdlt = 50.0] rad/sec.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: disablement of engine pre-positioning algorithm during engine stop events
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: Possibly rougher/longer engine starts for a very discerning customer - no telltale illuminated

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Excessive Clutch "A" Slippage

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Time after powerup is greater than cal threshold, PSR voltage is within normal range, motor speed, generator speed, and ECM's CAN engine speed signal are okay to use (no faults), and calculated engine speed (using motor/generator speed) is above min threshold - 500rpm.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: Difference between ECM's engine speed and HCU calculated engine speed is greater than CAL threshold ([pvp_damper_slpdlt = 52.400002] rad/sec) for 500ms continuously.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: Maximum engine torque and speed will be limited for the rest of the power cycle - customer may notice reduced performance.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: HEV Wrench lamp, and customer may notice reduce performance

DIDS_SUPPORTED_BY_SNAPSHOT.DID_REFS: did_DD00 did_DD01 did_DD02 did_DD05 did_032B did_1E00 did_1E12 did_1E23 did_1E26 did_1E2C did_480B did_480D did_4819 did_481B did_481D did_481E did_481F did_4820 did_4821 did_4822 did_4824 did_4826 did_C020 did_C024 did_D000 did_D008 did_D112 did_D115 did_D70A did_D703 did_F405 did_FD77 did_FD78 did_FD79 did_4823 did_C01D did_C01E

DIDS_SUPPORTED_BY_FREEZE_FRAME.DID_REFS: did_F404 did_F405 did_F40C did_F40D did_F41F did_F430 did_F431 did_F442 did_F449

DTC.DESCRIPTION: TCM Power Relay Control Circuit Low

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Time after powerup is greater than cal threshold, and PSR voltage is within cal range. Details: Entry conditions: #1, #2, and #3 below must be TRUE in order to run the test. Entry conditions: 1) Powerup timer is GREATER than calibrateable value [hld_psr_fb_putmr] seconds. 2) PSR voltage (hardwire) is GREATER than min PSR voltage for fault detection (9.0V) 3) 12V power supply high fault is not set
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: Test fails if PSR relay is commanded Open, no other hardwired circuits are present to keep the PSR closed, and the module is still running - this indicates a short to ground on the control line. Fault conditions: Module Wakeup hardwired signal = FALSE AND HEV Power sustain relay command = OPEN AND Module Key-Run hardwired signal = FALSE
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: No further action taken by this fault detection
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: Module will not be able to powerdown successfully, possibly leading to 12V battery issues. HEV Repair (Wrench) light will be illuminated

DIDS_SUPPORTED_BY_SNAPSHOT.DID_REFS: did_DD00 did_DD01 did_DD02 did_DD05 did_032B did_1E00 did_1E12 did_1E23 did_1E26 did_1E2C did_480B did_480D did_4819 did_481B did_481D did_481E did_481F did_4820 did_4821 did_4822 did_4824 did_4826 did_C020 did_C024 did_D000 did_D008 did_D112 did_D115 did_D70A did_D703 did_F405 did_FD77 did_FD78 did_FD79 did_4823 did_C01D did_C01E

DIDS_SUPPORTED_BY_FREEZE_FRAME.DID_REFS: did_F404 did_F405 did_F40C did_F40D did_F41F did_F430 did_F431 did_F442 did_F449

DTC.DESCRIPTION: TCM Power Relay Control Circuit High

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Time after powerup is greater than cal threshold, and PSR voltage is within cal range. Two independant faults (hld_flt_psr_fb and vmp_nopsp_flt) may generate this DTC. Details for hld_flt_psr_fb fault: Logical Entry conditions: #1, #2, and #3 below must be TRUE in order to run the test. 1) Powerup timer is GREATER than calibrateable value [hld_psr_fb_putmr] seconds. 2) PSR voltage (hardwire) is GREATER than min PSR voltage for fault detection (9.0V) 3) 12V power supply high fault is not set Details for vmp_nopsp_flt fault: Logical Entry conditions: Can only be set at module powerup, checked only once during powerup sequence
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: Test fails if PSR relay is commanded closed, and output feedback signal is faulted, or normal shutdown was not completed Fault conditions for hld_flt_psr_fb fault: 1) PSR OUT relay command = CLOSED 2) HEV Power sustain relay feedback fault present is TRUE Fault conditions for vmp_nopsp_flt fault: 1) Normal shutdown was not completed on previous power cycle
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: Action in response to hld_flt_psr_fb fault - No action taken Action in respionse to vmp_nopsp_flt fault - No action taken
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: This fault will usually generate a module reset. HEV Repair (Wrench) light will also illuminate

DIDS_SUPPORTED_BY_SNAPSHOT.DID_REFS: did_DD00 did_DD01 did_DD02 did_DD05 did_032B did_1E00 did_1E12 did_1E23 did_1E26 did_1E2C did_480B did_480D did_4819 did_481B did_481D did_481E did_481F did_4820 did_4821 did_4822 did_4824 did_4826 did_C020 did_C024 did_D000 did_D008 did_D112 did_D115 did_D70A did_D703 did_F405 did_FD77 did_FD78 did_FD79 did_4823 did_C01D did_C01E

DIDS_SUPPORTED_BY_FREEZE_FRAME.DID_REFS: did_F404 did_F405 did_F40C did_F40D did_F41F did_F430 did_F431 did_F442 did_F449

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Planetary Gear Set Failure

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Planetary gear lock fault runs if PSR voltage inside cal range, motor speed, gen speed, gen torque estimate, and ECM's engine speed all okay to use. Details: Logical Entry conditions (check_planetary_gear_lock(void)): All of the following Entry conditions must be TRUE in order to enter Entry state for this DTC. Entry conditions (check_planetary_gear_lock(void)): 1) PSR voltage is in normal range 2) Generator torque estimate NOT faulted 3) Motor speed NOT faulted 4) generator speed NOT faulted 5) Engine speed CAN signal NOT faulted
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: Planetary gear lock fault fails if:generator torque command above cal threshold, engine speed control error above cal threshold (large), and engine-generator speed difference below cal threshold (small). Engine Lock fault fails if: Both sources of engine speed indicate very low engine speed, Generator torque is high enough to expect rapid engine acceleration, and the generator is producing the torque that is commanded of it. Details: Logical Fault conditions: All of the following fault conditions must be TRUE in order to generate the DTC from check_planetary_gear_lock(void) function for this fault. Fault conditions (check_planetary_gear_lock(void)): 1) Generator torque command is GREATER than minimum (variable, at least 40Nm) 2) (error in engine speed control = engine speed (rad/sec) - HEV desired engine speed) is GREATER than minimum error in engine speed control (calibrateable) 3) (difference between engine and generator speeds = engine speed (rad/sec) - generator speed in rad/s from MCU) is LESS than maximum speed difference btwn planetary (calibrateable, [pvp_gearlock_n_dlt = 10.0])
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: Motor: NA, Generator: MGLOS_ZEROTQ, VVC: VVCLOS_OK - This fault detection will disable the generator, causing the vehicle to operate with the electric motor only - reduced vehicle performance and range. Definitions: MGLOS_ZEROTQ - Force the torque command to zero, but keep the inverter switching (operating). VVCLOS_OK - No action taken
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: on powersplit or BEV PDU HEV Hazard Lamp and LOS Electric operation and for plugin, MIL - check engine

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Test is run when PSR voltage is within valid range, the CAN PDB reports that the Hold Out Of Park (HOOP) solenoid is powered, and the HPCM is commanding the HOOP solenoid to be powered
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: Test fails when measured duty cycle of the low-side circuit of the HOOP solenoid is 0 while the HPCM is commanding a non-zero duty cycle.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: TBD
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: Hold out of park feature may not function or vehicle may be stuck out of park

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: Test fails when PKSL measured duty cycle is 100% but HPCM is commanded a valid duty cycle.

DTC.DESCRIPTION: High Voltage System Interlock Circuit "A"

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Details: Logical Entry conditions: All Entry conditions must be TRUE in order to enter an Entry state. Entry conditions: 1) Powerup timer value must be GREATER than a calibrated value [omp_hvint_putmr = 0.50] seconds. 2) 12V power MUST be in the normal operating range
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: 1. For DC interlock - if circuit is open, test fails. Fail conditions: 1) HV interlock shutdown circuit = OPEN 2) CAN signals from HV battery indicate Interlock failure (Fault flag for bcu_ilock_mode = FALSE) AND (CAN interlock mode = ILOCK_OPEN)
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: Motor and Generator will be latched to "zero torque" mode, Vehicle shutdown will result
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: on powersplit or BEV PDU HEV Hazard Warning Lamp - Red Triangle and for plugin MIL - check engine

DIDS_SUPPORTED_BY_SNAPSHOT.DID_REFS: did_DD00 did_DD01 did_DD02 did_DD05 did_032B did_1E00 did_1E12 did_1E23 did_1E26 did_1E2C did_480B did_480D did_4819 did_481B did_481D did_481E did_481F did_4820 did_4821 did_4822 did_4824 did_4826 did_C020 did_C024 did_D000 did_D008 did_D112 did_D115 did_D70A did_D703 did_F405 did_FD77 did_FD78 did_FD79 did_4823 did_C01D did_C01E

DTC.DESCRIPTION: DC/DC Converter Enable Circuit "A"/Open

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: HV Battery Contactors are closed (HV applied to vehicle), and vehicle is in ready to produce torque
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_PERIOD: 100ms - test only runs once at vehicle powerup
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: During VVC selftest (runs only at powerup) upper leg is determined "failed" if the high-side inverter voltage can not be controlled down to low-side HV bus voltage (battery voltage).
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: Disable VVC Boost converter - vehicle will run in electric mode only with no battery charging possible by powertrain.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: on powersplit or BEV PDU HEV Hazard - Red triangle and for plugin, MIL - check engine, Vehicle will have limited range and performance - only allow operation with electric motor and battery power - no regenerative braking, no generator or engine operation.

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Motor Torque Sensor Circuit Range/Performance

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Time after powerup is greater than cal threshold, PSR voltage is within cal range, and 2.5V reference power supply is OK
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_PERIOD: 10ms fault detection rate, 100ms DTC test rate
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: If MGCU motor current sensor reference voltage is outside of calibrateable range, test fails
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: Disable motor inverter - Vehicle can drive with engine/generator alone with severely reduced performance
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: on powersplit or BEV PDU HEV Hazard Warning Lamp - Red Triangle and for plugin, MIL - check engine

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Generator Control Module

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Time after powerup is greater than cal threshold ([trp_gtqcmd_putmr = 0.50] seconds), PSR voltage is within cal range, and generator torque estimate signal from MGCU can be trusted (no current sensor/resolver/voltage issues with inverter).
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: Two fault conditions can trigger this DTC: 1. Difference between generator torque estimate (from MGCU) and generator torque command (from HCU) is larger than cal threshold ([trp_gtqcmd_set = 40.0] Nm) for cal time ( 550ms ) . 2. Total estimated torque produced by motor and generator exceeds cal threshold (800Nm) in the wrong direction of driver torque demand.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: 1: Generator system shutdown - Vehicle can drive on electric power for limited distance. 2: Vehicle shutdown.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: on powersplit or BEV PDU HEV Hazard Warning Lamp - Red Triangle and for plugin, MIL - check engine

DIDS_SUPPORTED_BY_SNAPSHOT.DID_REFS: did_DD00 did_DD01 did_DD02 did_DD05 did_032B did_1E00 did_1E12 did_1E23 did_1E26 did_1E2C did_480B did_480D did_4819 did_481B did_481D did_481E did_481F did_4820 did_4821 did_4822 did_4824 did_4826 did_C020 did_C024 did_D000 did_D008 did_D112 did_D115 did_D70A did_D703 did_F405 did_FD77 did_FD78 did_FD79 did_4823 did_C01D did_C01E

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Drive Motor "A" Control Module Performance

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Time after powerup is greater than cal threshold [trp_mtqcmd_putmr = 0.50], 12V power ok, and motor torque estimate signal from MGCU can be trusted (no current sensor/resolver/voltage issues with inverter).
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: Two fault conditions can trigger this DTC: 1. Difference between motor torque estimate (from MGCU) and motor torque command (from HCU) is larger than cal threshold [trp_mtqcmd_set = 60.0] Nm for cal time ( 550ms ) . 2. Total estimated torque produced by motor and generator exceeds cal threshold (800Nm) in the wrong direction of driver torque demand.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: 1: Motor system shutdown - Vehicle can drive with engine/generator alone with severely reduced performance. 2: Vehicle shutdown.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: on powersplit or BEV PDU HEV Hazard Warning Lamp - Red Triangle and for plugin, MIL - check engine

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Hybrid/EV Powertrain Control Module "A"

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: MHT: PSR voltage is within cal range, 2.5V A/D reference volatge is within cal range Powersplit: PSR voltage is within cal range, 2.5V A/D reference volatge is within cal range, 5V A/D supply voltage is within cal range
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: Fail condition: inter-core communication failure detected for a calibrateable time ( 20ms) fault 199
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: MGCU temporary action: Powersplit:Zero torque applied on both motor and generator If the fault intermittently sets and clears more than 7 times in 14 seconds, the fault action becomes permanent. MHT: Zero torque applied on motor If the fault intermittently sets and clears more than 7 times in 14 seconds, the fault action becomes permanent.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: on powersplit or BEV PDU HEV Hazard warning Lamp - Red Triangle

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Generator Torque Sensor Circuit Range/Performance

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Powersplit: Time after powerup is greater than cal threshold, PSR voltage is within cal range, 2.5V A/D reference voltage is IN RANGE, 5V A/D supply voltage is within cal range MHT: Time after powerup is greater than cal threshold, PSR voltage is within cal range, 2.5V A/D reference voltage is IN RANGE
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_PERIOD: MGCU: 1ms fault detection rate, 100ms DTC test rate
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: If MGCU generator current sensor reference supply voltage is outside of calibrateable range for a calibrateable time ( 500ms) (Powerplit: higher than 5.3V or lower than 4.7V ; MHT: higher than 5.315V or lower than 4.691V), test fails fault 236 (MHT) fault 237 (Powersplit)
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: No action other than setting a code for reporting. Powersplit: ??? Disable generator inverter - Vehicle will operate on Electric power alone (no engine operation) MHT: ??? Disable generator inverter - Vehicle will operate on Engine alone This action persists as long as the fault persists
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: On powersplit or BEV PDU HEV Hazard Warning Lamp - Red Triangle and for plugin, MIL - check engine

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Drive Motor "A" Temperature Sensor "A" Circuit Range/Performance

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Time after powerup is within cal range, motor/generator coil temp sensors and oil temp sensor not faulted, and vehicle has sat with key off for cal threshold time. Details: Logical Entry conditions: All of the following Entry conditions must be TRUE in order to enter an Entry state. Entry conditions: 1) Powerup timer is GREATER than threshold for Powerup timer to check fault (calibrateable, [ldp_oilcoil_putmr] seconds) 2) Powerup timer is LESS than maximum time after powerup to run test (calibrateable, [ldp_oilcoil_maxtm] seconds) 3) Generator coil temp circuit - no faults 4) Motor coil temp circuit - no faults 5) Key has been off, vehicle powered down for longer than cal time (calibrateable, [ldp_oilcoil_soak] minutes) 6) Key off timer not faulted 7) Transmission oil temp circuit - no faults 8) 5V reference voltage is ok
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: Temperature difference between motor coil temp sensor and other two sensors (oil and gen coil) is greater than cal threshold, while difference between other two sensors is small Detailed Fault condition: (Transmission oil temperature - motor coil winding temperature) is GREATER than maximum threshold (calibrateable, [ldp_m_oilcoil_lim] deg C) AND (Transmission oil temperature - generator coil winding temperature) is LESS than maximum threshold (calibrateable, [ldp_oilcoil_ok_dlt] deg C) AND (Motor coil winding temperature - generator coil winding temperature) is GREATER than maximum threshold (calibrateable, [ldp_m_oilcoil_lim] deg C)
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: No additional action taken by fault detection, however torque reduction could result if sensor has faulted in-range high. This torque reduction may result in more engine run time.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: FHEV: yellow wrench, PHEV: Check Engine (MIL). More engine run time may occur.

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Drive Motor "A" Temperature Sensor "A" Circuit Low

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Powerup timer greater than cal threshold, PSR voltage within Cal range, 2.5V reference is ok, 5V reference is ok, and vehicle mode is NOT VM_INIT Details: Logical Entry conditions: All of the following Entry conditions must be TRUE in order to enter fault detection. Entry conditions: 1) Powerup timer value must be GREATER than the a calibrateable value [hld_mcoil_putmr] seconds XXX:MGCU fault 2) 2.5V reference voltage is measured inside acceptable range 3) 5V reference voltage is within range 4) LV fault - PSR high 12V must be FALSE 5) LV fault - PSR low 12V must be FALSE 6) Vehicle mode is NOT VM_INIT
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: Test fails if voltage from motor coil temp sensor is below critical voltage (calibrateable, [mtr_coiltemp_raw_v_min (0.1V) ]) for detecting a short to ground (HCU).
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: No further action is taken by this fault except for more conservative overtemperature protection during hill-hold manuevers - motor will derate torque sooner with this fault present if driver attempts to hold zero speed on steep incline by using accel pedal only.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: FHEV: yellow wrench, PHEV: Check Engine (MIL)

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Drive Motor "A" Temperature Sensor "A" Circuit High

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Powerup timer greater than cal threshold, PSR voltage within Cal range, 2.5V reference is ok, 5V reference is ok, and vehicle mode is NOT VM_INIT Details: Logical Entry conditions: All of the following Entry conditions must be TRUE in order to enter fault detection. Entry conditions: 1) Powerup timer value must be GREATER than the a calibrateable value [hld_mcoil_putmr] 2) 2.5V reference voltage is measured inside acceptable range 3) 5V reference voltage is within range 4) LV fault - PSR high 12V must be FALSE 5) LV fault - PSR low 12V must be FALSE 6) Vehicle mode is NOT VM_INIT
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: Test fails if voltage from motor coil temp sensor is above critical voltage (calibrateable, [mtr_coiltemp_raw_v_max] V ) AND trans oil temperature (hardwire) is GREATER than cal [hld_tempsens_flt_floor] 15 deg C AND generator coil temperature (hardwire) is GREATER than cal [hld_tempsens_flt_floor] 15 deg C
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: No further action is taken by this fault except for more conservative overtemperature protection during hill-hold manuevers - motor will derate torque sooner with this fault present if driver attempts to hold zero speed on steep incline by using accel pedal only. This may result in more engine run time.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: FHEV: yellow wrench, PHEV: Check Engine (MIL). More engine run time may occur.

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Drive Motor "A" Temperature Sensor "A" Circuit Intermittent

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Time after powerup is greater than threshold (0.5 seconds) and 12V PSR voltage inside required range for module operation.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_PERIOD: Test runs every 2ms, DTC is evaluated every 100ms
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: Test fails if motor temp sensor rate of change exceeds a threshold (50 degC/sec) for at least 5 samples
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: Machine torque derating will use a default value, max component speeds can be reduced to fixed thresholds, and cooling system requests will be max (pump flow, fan speeds, etc)
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: No warning lights illuminated, potential small decreases in max performance.

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Drive Motor "A" Over Temperature

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Time after PowerUp is greater than cal, 5V ref voltage is in range, and motor coil temp, inverter temp, and oil temp sensors not shorted or open circuit
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: Either one of the following being true will generate this DTC: 1) generator maximum torque derating due to elevated temperatures is greater than 50% for 2 seconds 2) motor maximum torque derating due to elevated temperatures is greater than 50% for 2 seconds
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: No further action is taken by fault detection, but reduced vehicle performance will be noticeable
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: Overtemperature Lamp

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Generator Temperature Sensor Circuit Range/Performance

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Time after powerup is within cal range, motor/generator coil temp sensors and oil temp sensor not faulted, and vehicle has sat with key off for cal threshold time. Details: Logical Entry conditions: All of the following Entry conditions must be TRUE in order to enter the Entry state for this DTC. Entry conditions: 1) Powerup timer is GREATER than threshold for Powerup timer to check fault (calibrateable, [ldp_oilcoil_putmr] sec ) 2) Powerup timer is LESS than maximum time after powerup to run test (calibrateable, [ldp_oilcoil_maxtm] sec ) 3) Generator coil temp circuit - not faulted 4) Motor coil temp circuit - not faulted 5) Vehicle has sat with power off for minimum time (calibrateable, [ldp_oilcoil_soak] minutes ) 6) Key off timer not faulted 7) Transmission oil temp circuit - not faulted 8) 5V reference voltage within range
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: Temperature difference between generator coil temp sensor and other two sensors (oil and motor coil) is greater than cal threshold, while difference between other two sensors is less than smaller cal threshold Fault condition: 1) (Transmission oil temperature - generator coil winding temperature) is GREATER than maximum threshold (calibrateable, [ldp_g_oilcoil_lim] deg C) AND (Transmission oil temperature - motor coil winding temperature) is LESS than maximum threshold (calibrateable, [ldp_oilcoil_ok_dlt] deg C) AND (Motor coil winding temperature - generator coil winding temperature) is GREATER than maximum threshold (calibrateable, [ldp_g_oilcoil_lim] deg C)
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: No further actions taken by this fault detection
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: FHEV: yellow wrench, PHEV: Check Engine (MIL)

MHT: Powersplit: Time after powerup is greater than cal threshold, PSR voltage is within normal range and 2.5V A/D reference voltage is within normal range, 5V temperature sensor reference voltage is measured inside the acceptable range
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: Test fails if voltage from generator coil temp sensor is below critical voltage (calibrateable value, [Powersplit: voltage lower than 0.03V, MHT: voltage lower than 0.02V]) for detecting a short to ground for a calibrateable time ( 40ms) - fault 278 (MHT) -fault 279 (Powersplit)
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: MGCU: No action will be taken for this fault detection other than reporting.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: FHEV: yellow wrench, PHEV: Check Engine (MIL)

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_PERIOD: MGCU: 10ms fault detection rate, 100ms DTC test
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: Test fails if voltage from generator coil temperature sensor is above critical voltage (calibrateable, [Powersplit: sensor voltage higher than 4.97V, MHT: sensor voltage higher than 4.937V ]) for detecting an open circuit or short to power for a calibrateable time ( 2000ms) - fault 276 (MHT) -fault 277 (Powersplit)
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: MGCU: No further actions taken by the fault detection other than reporting.

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Drive Motor "A" Inverter Over Temperature

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: PowerUp timer is greater than cal ([ldp_derating_putmr] seconds), mot/gen inv temperature circuit ok, inductor temperature ok, upper/lower IGBT temperature ok, and no faults detected this 12V power cycle with Motor Electronics Cooling Pump (MECP) or related circuitry.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: Derating factor for vvc igbt temps is lower than cal threshold [ldp_vvc_ot_thr = 0.10] OR derating factor for inductor temp is lower than cal threshold [ldp_vvc_ot_thr = 0.10] OR derating factor for gen inverter temp is lower than cal threshold [ldp_inv_ot_thr = 0.250] OR derating factor for mtr inverter temp is lower than cal threshold [ldp_inv_ot_thr = 0.250]
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: No further action taken by fault detection
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: Overtemperature Lamp

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Drive Motor "A" Position Sensor Circuit

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Test runs all the time, no entry conditions
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: 1. Hardwired fault line from R2D chip indicates motor resolver failure to MGCU processor for 250msec continuously 2. Hardwired fault line from R2D chip is intermittently setting and clearing more than 5 times in 5 seconds.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: The motor inverter will be temporarily disabled while the resolver hardware is reset in an attempt to get the error to clear. If the error does not clear, a cal number of resets will be attempted, and the inverter will remain disabled. DTC will set after 250ms of continuous inverter disablement, or excessive toggling between enabled/disabled.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: FHEV: yellow wrench, PHEV: MIL - check engine, Momentary reduction in powertrain torque output

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Drive Motor "A" Position Sensor Circuit Range/Performance

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: (No Motor Resolver Error Fault) AND (Resolver Serial Link Fault is not set and check is complete) AND ( |Motor Speed| greater than 0) AND (Motor Over Current Fault is set)
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: MGCU fault 262 Fault conditions: ABZ reading and SPI RD reading difference greater than 3.6 degrees for more than 20ms. The position of the motor is not being read correctly.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: Disable Motor inverter
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: Hazard (red triangle) light and LOS No-Motor operation and for plugin, MIL - check engine, Vehicle can operate with reduced performance using only generator and engine.

DIDS_SUPPORTED_BY_SNAPSHOT.DID_REFS: did_DD00 did_DD01 did_DD02 did_DD05 did_032B did_1E00 did_1E12 did_1E23 did_1E26 did_1E2C did_480B did_480D did_4819 did_481B did_481D did_481E did_481F did_4820 did_4821 did_4822 did_4824 did_4826 did_C020 did_C024 did_D000 did_D008 did_D112 did_D115 did_D70A did_D703 did_F405 did_FD77 did_FD78 did_FD79 did_4823 did_C01D did_C01E

DIDS_SUPPORTED_BY_FREEZE_FRAME.DID_REFS: did_F404 did_F405 did_F40C did_F40D did_F41F did_F430 did_F431 did_F442 did_F449

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Drive Motor "A" Position Sensor Circuit Overspeed

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Time after power is greater than cal threshold [ldp_mspdhi_putmr] seconds or [ldp_mspdunc_putmr] seconds, and no motor resolver related faults from MGCU.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: If Motor speed is above cal threshold for cal amount of time, fault will set. Details: Fault conditions: Any one or more of the faults below (#1, #2, #3, or #4) being TRUE will generate a DTC for this fault. Faults are based on the following conditions: 1) Filtered motor speed is GREATER than motor rps to set the fault (calibrateable, [ldp_mspdhi_lim = 1822.119995] rad/sec for 10ms) 2) Filtered motor speed is GREATER than motor rps to set the fault (calibrateable, [ldp_mspdunc_lim = 1790.0] rad/sec for 100ms)
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: Both of these faults will temporarily disable the motor and generator inverter - temporary loss of powertrain torque
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: No lamp illumination, but temporary loss of torque

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Time after powerup is greater than cal threshold [200ms]
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_PERIOD: MGCU: 1ms fault detection rate, 100ms DTC test
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: Resolver Hardwired fault reported by R2D chip indicates generator resolver failure to MGCU processor for 10ms continuously fault 256 (MHT) fault 257 (Powersplit)
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: The generator inverter will be temporarily disabled. If the fault intermittently sets and clears more than 7 times in 14 seconds, the fault action becomes permanent. DTC will set after 250ms of continuous inverter disablement, or excessive toggling between enabled/disabled.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: FHEV: yellow wrench, PHEV: MIL - check engine light, Momentary reduction in powertrain torque output

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_PERIOD: MGCU: 1ms fault detection rate, 100ms DTC test
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: 1. Hardwired fault lines from resolver fault detection circuitry indicate resolver failure to MGCU processor for 10ms continuously 2. Hardwired fault lines from resolver fault detection circuitry is intermittently setting and clearing more than 5 times in 5 seconds. fault 258 (Powersplit) fault 259 (Powersplit)
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: The generator inverter will be temporarily disabled while the resolver hardware is reset in an attempt to get the error to clear. If the error does not clear, a cal number of resets will be attempted, and the inverter will remain disabled. DTC will set after 250ms of continuous inverter disablement, or excessive toggling between enabled/disabled.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: TBD

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Generator Position Sensor Circuit Range/Performance

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: (Time after powerup is greater than cal threshold [300ms]) AND (No R2D chip reported Generator Resolver Error Fault) AND ("No Generator Resolver circuitry and R2D chip Error" Fault) AND (Resolver Serial Link Fault is not set after check is complete) AND (Generator 3-Phase Over Current Fault is set)
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: Fault conditions: ABZ reading and SPI RD reading difference greater than 3.6 degrees for more than 20ms. The position of the generator is not being read correctly. - fault 262 (MHT) - fault 263 (Powersplit)
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: MGCU: No action is taken for this fault other than reporting (its predecessor over current takes an action)
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: Hazard (red triangle) light and LOS Electric operation and for plugin, MIL - check engine, Vehicle can operate with reduced performance using only Electric motor

DIDS_SUPPORTED_BY_FREEZE_FRAME.DID_REFS: did_F404 did_F405 did_F40C did_F40D did_F41F did_F430 did_F431 did_F442 did_F449

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Generator Position Sensor Circuit Overspeed

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Time after power is greater than cal threshold, and no generator resolver related faults from MGCU. Details: Logical Entry conditions: Logical equation is (#1 AND #2) OR (#3 AND #2), so Entry state will result if either #1 AND #2 are TRUE or #3 AND #2 are TRUE. Entry conditions: 1) Powerup timer is GREATER than threshold for a Powerup timer (calibrateable, [ldp_gspdhi_putmr] seconds) 2) No generator resolver faults reported by Motor Control processor 3) Powerup timer is GREATER than threshold for a Powerup timer (calibrateable, [ldp_gspdunc_putmr] seconds)
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: If Generator speed is above cal threshold for cal amount of time, fault will set. Details: Logical fault conditions: Any one or more of the following faults (#1, #2, #3, or #4) being true will generate DTC. Fault conditions: 3) generator speed in rad/s from MCU is GREATER the generator rps to set the fault (calibrateable, [ldp_gspdhi_lim = 1895.430054] rad/sec for 10ms) 4) generator speed in rad/s from MCU is GREATER than generator rps to set the fault (calibrateable, [ldp_gspdunc_lim = 1623.156006] rad/sec for 10ms)
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: Both faults will result in temporary generator inverter disablement. Temporary loss of generator and engine functionality.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: FHEV: yellow wrench, PHEV: no warning indicator, Possible engine speed flares for short periods of time

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Drive Motor "A" Phase U Current

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Time after powerup is greater than cal threshold, PSR voltage is within cal range, and sensor reference voltage is inside normal range
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: If sensor offset is greater than cal threshold (30 amps), MGCU fault motor U current sensor offset - fault 224
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: Motor: MGLOS_DISABLE_IPU, Generator: MGLOS_OK, VVC: VVCLOS_OK Definitions: MGLOS_DISABLE_IPU - Permanently turn off all switches in the inverter. This means there will be zero DC power usage, and zero 3-phase current flowing in the motor/generator. This mode is NOT recoverable even if the fault clears (for that power cycle). MGLOS_OK - Take no action VVCLOS_OK - No action taken
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: on powersplit or BEV PDU HEV Hazard Lamp - red triangle, vehicle shutdown/no-start and for plugin, MIL - check engine as well

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: If current sensor in motor inverter reads a current outside the normal range, test fails. MGCU fault motor U current sensor - over current - fault 238
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: Motor: MGLOS_TEMP_DISABLE_IPU, Generator: MGLOS_OK, VVC: VVCLOS_OK Definitions: MGLOS_TEMP_DISABLE_IPU - Temporarily turn off all switches in the inverter. This means there will be zero DC power usage, and zero 3-phase current flowing in the motor/generator. This mode is recoverable if the fault clears. MGLOS_OK - Take no action VVCLOS_OK - No action taken
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: FHEV: yellow wrench, PHEV: Check Engine (MIL), Momentary wheel torque reduction.

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Generator Phase U Current

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Powersplit: Time after powerup is greater than cal threshold, PSR voltage is within cal range, 2.5V A/D reference volatge is within cal range, , 5V A/D supply voltage is within cal range, current sensor zeroing is done and current sensor reference voltage is inside normal range MHT: Time after powerup is greater than cal threshold, PSR voltage is within cal range, 2.5V A/D reference volatge is within cal range, current sensor zeroing is done and current sensor reference voltage is inside normal range
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_PERIOD: MGCU: detection at power up and then at a rate of 1ms 100ms
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: If current sensor offset is greater than cal threshold (Generator: 18A in powersplit, 9.5A in MHT) during MGCU powerup, MGCU fault generator U current sensor offset - fault 224 (MHT) - fault 225 (Powersplit)

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Drive Motor "A" Inverter Performance

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: PSR voltage is within cal range, and MGCU operating mode is NOT "Terminate" - powered down
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: Test fails if any of these fault condtions are true: 1) Inverter hardwired IGBT fault signal is received, indicating IGBT failure/automatic shutdown of inverter - fault 288. 2) Hardwired fault indication toggles on/off excessively
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: Fault detection will not take any further action, but Motor will be temporarily disabled. Abrupt, short interruptions of torque may be noticed by driver. If motor is temporarily disabled long enough, the system will permanently disable motor inverter, and set another DTC to indicate this further action.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: FHEV: yellow wrench, PHEV: Check Engine (MIL)

DIDS_SUPPORTED_BY_SNAPSHOT.DID_REFS: did_DD00 did_DD01 did_DD02 did_DD05 did_032B did_1E00 did_1E12 did_1E23 did_1E26 did_1E2C did_480B did_480D did_4819 did_481B did_481D did_481E did_481F did_4820 did_4821 did_4822 did_4824 did_4826 did_C020 did_C024 did_D000 did_D008 did_D112 did_D115 did_D70A did_D703 did_F405 did_FD77 did_FD78 did_FD79 did_4823 did_C01D did_C01E

DIDS_SUPPORTED_BY_FREEZE_FRAME.DID_REFS: did_F404 did_F405 did_F40C did_F40D did_F41F did_F430 did_F431 did_F442 did_F449

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Generator Inverter Performance

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Time after powerup is greater than cal threshold, PSR voltage is within cal range and MGCU operating mode is not in power down mode
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_PERIOD: MGCU: 100us fault detection rate; 1ms fault reporting rate. - 100ms DTC test
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: Test fails if inverter hardwired IGBT fault signal is received, indicating IGBT failure/automatic shutdown of inverter. (H/w takes immediate action.) DTC will also trigger if this hardwired fault indication toggles on/off excessively - fault 288 (MHT) - fault 289 (Powersplit)
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: Powersplit: Generator will be temporarily disabled. Short Engine speed flares and reduction in powertrain torque may be noticed by driver. If generator is temporarily disabled long enough, the system will disable generator inverter for that key cycle, and set another DTC to indicate this further action. If the fault intermittently sets and clears more than 7 times in 14 seconds, the fault action becomes permanent. MHT: Generator will be temporarily disabled. If generator is temporarily disabled long enough, the system will disable generator inverter for that key cycle, and set another DTC to indicate this further action. If the fault intermittently sets and clears more than 7 times in 14 seconds, the fault action becomes permanent.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: FHEV: yellow wrench, PHEV: MIL - check engine

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Hybrid/EV Battery Pack State of Charge Low

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Vehicle is in Passive Neutral operation mode, AND Vehicle mode is not Initializing or Power-Up, for a cal amount of time
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: Hybrid Battery Pack State of Charge below cal threshold
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: Vehicle Shutdown
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: on powersplit or BEV PDU HEV Hazard Light (Red Triangle) and vehicle shutdown

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Drive Motor "A" Performance

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: PSR voltage in cal range, and motor is in a torque producing mode (vehicle successfully started)
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: Fault #248 received from MGCU - motor current feedback error - Difference between commanded motor current and measured motor current exceeds 35 amps for 500msec.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: Definitions: MGLOS_DISABLE_IPU - Permanently turn off all switches in the inverter. This means there will be zero DC power usage, and zero 3-phase current flowing in the motor/generator. This mode is NOT recoverable even if the fault clears (for that power cycle). MGLOS_OK - Take no action VVCLOS_OK - No action taken Motor: MGLOS_DISABLE_IPU, Generator: MGLOS_OK, VVC: VVCLOS_OK
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: on powersplit or BEV PDU HEV Hazard lamp and LOS No motor operation and for plugin, MIL - check engine, Vehicle operation continues with generator and engine with reduced performance.

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Hybrid Generator Performance

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: PSR voltage in cal range, and generator is in a torque producing mode (vehicle successfully started)
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: Fault #249 received from MGCU - generator current feedback error - Difference between commanded generator current and measured generator current exceeds 35 amps for 500msec.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: Definitions: MGLOS_DISABLE_IPU - Permanently turn off all switches in the inverter. This means there will be zero DC power usage, and zero 3-phase current flowing in the motor/generator. This mode is NOT recoverable even if the fault clears (for that power cycle). MGLOS_OK - Take no action VVCLOS_OK - No action taken Motor: MGLOS_OK, Generator: MGLOS_DISABLE_IPU, VVC: VVCLOS_OK

DTC.DESCRIPTION: DC/DC Converter "A" Performance

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: All of the following conditions must be TRUE to run this test: 1. Time after powerup more than CAL (0.5 seconds) 2. 12V PSR voltage in range ( between 6.0V and 19.0V) 3. No circuit failures detected for inductor current, inverter AC current, motor/gen position, or input voltage sensors. 4. Inverter input voltage greater than 152 V (CAL) 5. Traction motor speed below 4000rpm 6. Total absolute power of motor and generator below CAL threshold (100kW) 7. No significant differences (>30Nm) between commanded and estimated motor or generator torques 8. Motor and Generator not in any Limited Operating Mode 9. VVC converter not actively boosting inverter voltage
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: Test fails if measured inductor current is more than 50 amps different from the calculated inductor current model. The currents must be different for at least 4 seconds continuously.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: No direct action taken.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: FHEV: no fault lamp, PHEV: MIL - check engine

DIDS_SUPPORTED_BY_SNAPSHOT.DID_REFS: did_DD00 did_DD01 did_DD02 did_DD05 did_032B did_1E00 did_1E12 did_1E23 did_1E26 did_1E2C did_480B did_480D did_4819 did_481B did_481D did_481E did_481F did_4820 did_4821 did_4822 did_4824 did_4826 did_C020 did_C024 did_D000 did_D008 did_D112 did_D115 did_D70A did_D703 did_F405 did_FD77 did_FD78 did_FD79 did_4823 did_C01D did_C01E

DIDS_SUPPORTED_BY_FREEZE_FRAME.DID_REFS: did_F404 did_F405 did_F40C did_F40D did_F41F did_F430 did_F431 did_F442 did_F449

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Drive Motor Inverter Temperature Sensor "A" Circuit Range/Performance

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: All of the following conditions must be TRUE to run this test: 1. Time after powerup more than CAL (3 seconds) 2. Time after powerup less than CAL2 (6 seconds) 3. Vehicle soak time (time spent in non-torque-producing mode) > 200minutes 4. Soak Time measurement is validated OK to use 5. PCM's ECT measurement received in range. 6. Both Motor and Generator torque command below CAL threshold (50 Nm)
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: Test fails if motor inverter temperature is more than 30 degC different from PCM's ECT measurement after the required soak time. Temperature must be different for at least 700ms continuously.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: No direct action taken.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: FHEV: no fault lamp, PHEV: MIL - check engine

DIDS_SUPPORTED_BY_FREEZE_FRAME.DID_REFS: did_F404 did_F405 did_F40C did_F40D did_F41F did_F430 did_F431 did_F442 did_F449

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Drive Motor Inverter Temperature Sensor "A" Circuit Low

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Time after powerup is greater than cal threshold, and internal voltages are within cal ranges. Details: Logical Entry conditions: All of the following Entry conditions must be TRUE in order to enter the Entry state for this DTC. Entry conditions: 1) Powerup timer value must be GREATER than the a calibrateable value [hld_minv_t_putmr 0.5 sec] XXX:MGCU fault 2) 2.5V reference voltage is measured inside acceptable range 3) LV fault - PSR high 12V must be FALSE 4) LV fault - PSR low 12V must be FALSE
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: Test fails if: 1) Inverter temperature sensor voltage falls below cal threshold (calibrateable, [hld_minv_t_vmin 0.779 Volts]). 2) MGCU fault motor gate signal shorted. - fault 220
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: No actions taken by fault detection, but vehicle will exhibit torque derating sooner when attempting hill-hold manuever - attempting to hold vehicle stationary on a steep grade using only accel pedal input. The vehicle's cooling fan will also run on High during this fault detection, and the inverter cooling pump will run continuously.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: FHEV: yellow wrench, PHEV: Check Engine (MIL)

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Drive Motor Inverter Temperature Sensor "A" Circuit High

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Time after powerup is greater than cal threshold, and internal voltages are within cal ranges. Details: Logical Entry conditions: All of the following Entry conditions must be TRUE in order to enter an Entry state for this DTC. Entry conditions: 1) Powerup timer value must be GREATER than the a calibrateable value [hld_minv_t_putmr 0.5 sec] XXX:MGCU fault 2) 2.5V reference voltage is measured inside acceptable range 3) LV fault - PSR high 12V must be FALSE 4) LV fault - PSR low 12V must be FALSE 5) vehicle mode is not VM_INIT
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: Details: Fault conditions: 1) If inverter temp sensor voltage is above cal threshold (calibrateable, [hld_minv_t_vmax 4.56V ]) 2) MGCU fault motor gate signal open - fault 218
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: No actions taken by fault detection, but vehicle will exhibit torque derating sooner when attempting hill-hold manuever - attempting to hold vehicle stationary on a steep grade using only accel pedal input. The vehicle's cooling fan will also run on High during this fault detection, and the inverter cooling pump will run continuously.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: FHEV: yellow wrench, PHEV: Check Engine (MIL)

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Drive Motor Inverter Temperature Sensor "A" Circuit Intermittent/Erratic

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Time after powerup is greater than threshold (0.5 seconds) and 12V PSR voltage inside required range for module operation.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_PERIOD: Fault is evaluated at 2ms rate, DTC is evaluated at 100ms
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: Test fails if motor inverter temp sensor rate of change exceeds a threshold (50 degC/sec) for at least 5 samples
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: Machine torque derating will use a default value, max component speeds can be reduced to fixed thresholds, and cooling system requests will be max (pump flow, fan speeds, etc)
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: No warning lights illuminated, potential small decreases in max performance.

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Electric/Auxiliary Transmission Fluid Pump Motor Control Module

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Time after powerup is greater than cal threshold, PSR voltage is within cal range, and Vehicle mode is past INIT - KEY is in run position, and TAOP communication (PWM) has been established
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: TAOP PWM feedback indicates an over-speed or under-speed condition for more than 2 seconds
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: TAOP is assumed disabled, engine may be forced to run to provide lubrication to transmission
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: FHEV: yellow wrench, PHEV: Check Engine (MIL), Possible increased engine-run time

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Generator Inverter Temperature Sensor "A" Circuit Range/Performance

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: All of the following conditions must be TRUE to run this test: 1. Time after powerup greater than CAL (3 seconds) 2. Time after powerup less than CAL2 (6 seconds) 3. Vehicle soak time (time spent in non-torque-producing mode) > 200minutes 4. Soak Time measurement is validated OK to use 5. PCM's ECT measurement received in range. 6. Both Motor and Generator torque command below CAL threshold (50 Nm)
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: Test fails if motor inverter temperature is more than 30 degC different from PCM's ECT measurement after the required soak time. Temperature must be different for at least 700ms continuously.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: No direct action taken.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: FHEV: no fault warning, PHEV: MIL - Check Engine

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Generator Inverter Temperature Sensor "A" Circuit Low

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Powersplit: Time after powerup is greater than cal threshold, PSR voltage is within normal range and 2.5V A/D reference voltage is within normal range, 5V A/D supply voltage is within cal range. MHT: Time after powerup is greater than cal threshold, PSR voltage is within normal range and 2.5V A/D reference voltage is within normal range.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_PERIOD: MGCU: 10ms fault detection rate, 100ms DTC test
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: Test fails if: Powersplit - Inverter temperature sensor circuit opens or shorts to ground/power for a calibrateable time ( 500ms), Additional info: Inverter temperature reads -2000C MHT - Inverter temperature sensor circuit shorts to ground for a calibrateable time ( 50ms), MGCU fault generator Inverter Temperature Sensor "A" Circuit Low - fault 270 (MHT) fault 271 (Powersplit)
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: Actions taken by fault detection: MGCU only reports this fault. The vehicle's cooling fan will run on High during this fault detection, and the inverter cooling pump will run continuously.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: FHEV: yellow wrench, PHEV: MIL - check engine

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_PERIOD: MHT: 10ms fault detection rate, 100ms DTC test
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: Details: Fault conditions: MHT only- Inverter temperature sensor circuit open or short to power for a calibrateable time ( 100ms), will cause this fault fault 272 (MHT)
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: Actions taken include: MGCU only reports this fault. The vehicle's cooling fan will run on High during this fault detection, and the inverter cooling pump will run continuously.
